1.errorText: A father calls Children's Engineering Workshops for information. His younger daughter Lydia is four and will join the Tiny Engineers. In that group, one activity is to design a cover for an egg so it won't break when dropped. They also compete to build the highest tower. They build a car powered by a balloon. His older daughter Carly has just turned seven and will be in the Junior Engineers, for children aged six to eight. They build and program model vehicles and animals. Their competition is to build the longest bridge from recycled materials. They also make a five-minute movie using animation software, and build a robot. Classes cost £50 for five weeks and are held on Thursdays from ten to eleven at Fradstone Industrial Estate. Parking is free.

signpost.quote: The modern day philosopher Nassim Nicholas Taleb defines a Stoic as someone who transforms fear into caution, pain into transformation.

1.errorText: A woman calls Picturerep about converting old family photos to digital format. Photos must be removed from their frame before sending. The minimum size is 4 centimetres and maximum is 30. The cost for 300–400 photos is £195 for the basic service, which includes one disk. Extra disks cost £5. Payment is due when the photos are sent. Photos can be kept in separate folders labelled by the customer. The company can improve colour, contrast, and can alter or remove objects or change the background. They cannot improve photos that are slightly out of focus. Processing takes ten days. Photos should be sent in a strong plastic box.

signpost.quote: It's well established that dance is a good way of encouraging adolescent girls to take exercise — but what about older people?

1.errorText: A woman calls Junior Cycle Camp for information about sending her seven-year-old son Charlie. Jake explains that Level 5 is for children like Charlie who can ride but need more training. They start practising on the site and then go to the park. All instructors wear red shirts, and supervisors wear blue shirts. Instructors must submit a reference from someone who has seen them teach cycling. Classes have a limit of ten children. During quiet times the instructor might tell a story or play a game. Classes still run in rain. Children should bring spare clothes, a snack, and shoes not sandals. On Day 1 children must arrive by 9.20, as there is a careful check that the helmet fits. The camp costs $199 per week.

1.scene: 谢尔维尔小屋农家度假预订

mcq.q: Why is Chervil Cottage cheaper than Granary Cottage?
opts: It is smaller — converted from a garage rather than a barn
It is on a lower floor

1.errorText: Tom calls to book Granary Cottage for the week beginning May 14th, but it's already taken. Shirley offers him Chervil Cottage from May 28th for £480. Chervil was built as a garage and is smaller than Granary Cottage. It has a double bedroom. The garden is accessed from the kitchen through French doors, with two deckchairs provided. Parking is available at the front. The cottage has a fridge-freezer, electric cooker, washing machine and television. There's a shower but no bath. There's central heating and a stove. Views from the bedroom include a famous stone bridge, and the living room looks toward the hills and a monument. The deposit is 30%, which is £144. The balance is due by the last day of April.

signpost.quote: For a long time it was believed that the dodo was slow and clumsy, but scientists now believe its strong knee joints would have made it capable of quite fast movement.
opts: Presenting new scientific evidence that overturns a long-held misconception about the dodo

signpost.quote: Another group of scientists carried out analysis of the dodo's skull. They found that the reports of the dodo's lack of intelligence were not borne out by their research.
opts: Introducing a second line of evidence that also contradicts the previous consensus

signpost.quote: It's hard to get an accurate description of what the dodo actually looked like — we have some written records but we don't know how reliable these are.
opts: Acknowledging the limitations of existing evidence before introducing specific examples

signpost.quote: So it looks as if the dodo was better able to survive and defend itself than was originally believed. Yet less than 200 years after Europeans first arrived, they became extinct.
opts: Creating a tension between new evidence of the dodo's capabilities and its rapid extinction

signpost.quote: We now think that probably the main cause of extinction was not the introduction of non-native species, but the introduction of agriculture.
opts: Presenting a surprising final conclusion that overturns another commonly held belief
